_comment: S5 Noise / Birdies — False-positive rate study (STUDY-SPEC §6).
name: Noise / birdies
description: Signal-free cycles. 30 trials per noise type (120 total slots). Drives: false-positive rate and between-appraiser agreement on false positives.
trials_note: Raised from 3 to 30 (12 -> 120 total slots) 2026-07-04, R&R-006 (GitHub #39). At the previous N=12, harness/analyse.py's MIN_N_FOR_FP_GATE (=49) meant this scenario could never clear the ratified R&R-004 95% Clopper-Pearson UB gate even at zero observed events -- every routine run reported the S5 metric as INFO, never a real PASS/FAIL. 120 matches the N already assumed by STUDY-SPEC.md Section 10's ratification text and worked examples (k=2 events -> UB 5.15% PASS; k=3 -> UB 6.33% FAIL), rather than introducing a new, undocumented sample size.
harness_note: No FT8 signal is synthesised. Each part renders a 15 s audio buffer containing only the specified interference. Any decode reported by either appraiser is a false positive.

parts.note: White (AWGN) noise at a moderate level — channel.py add_noise with no signal.
White (AWGN) noise at a higher level (hotter band).
Single steady CW carrier at the FT8 passband centre.
Multiple steady carriers ('birdies') spread across the FT8 passband.

channel_note: Pink noise and swept carriers are deferred — channel.py currently implements AWGN only. Extend channel.py before running S5 parts 0–1 vs 2–3 if pink/swept variants are added.
